title: get_adapter unknown-provider fallback hides typos by silently using OpenAI
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/providers/mod.rs:127-148
## Violation
get_adapter returns Box<dyn ProviderAdapter> with a warn-and-fallback for unknown providers:

  other => {
      tracing::warn!(provider = other, "Unknown provider — falling back to OpenAI-compatible adapter.");
      Box::new(OpenAIAdapter::new())
  }

The fallback is invoked at proxy.rs:1174 (adapter for passthrough auth headers) and pipeline.rs:246 (chat_endpoint construction). A typo in config.yaml's proxy.target ('anthrop1c' instead of 'anthropic') results in: OpenAI-shaped requests, OpenAI auth headers, but sent to the Anthropic base URL. The upstream returns 401 with no hint that the local config has a typo. The warn! is the only signal and may be lost in volume.
## Mandated refactor
Validate proxy.target at config load against the known-providers set; reject unknown values with a startup error. Or make get_adapter() return Result<Box<dyn ProviderAdapter>, ProviderError::Unknown(name)> and propagate at call sites. Silent fallback is acceptable only if the unknown-provider is explicitly listed as 'unknown-compatible'.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
